reasoning: The criteria require a "blockbuster" US movie (budget of $100M+) to be released in the US before December 31st 2025, and acknowledged as written by or significantly assisted by AI. The writers strike (WGA) in 2023 ended with strong contractual protections restricting the use of AI in union projects. A $100M budget implies a major studio production unequivocally subject to these strict WGA rules. Additionally, the forecast date is November 23, 2025, leaving only 38 days until the deadline. Any movie opening before tracking out of 2025 would already have completed its entire production and PR cycle, and no evidence exists of a $100M film making such an unprecedented acknowledgment. Doing so would violate WGA agreements and spark immense industry backlash. The few examples of AI used entirely for writing are incredibly low-budget indie/experimental shorts that don't approach the $100M threshold. Thus, the probability of this happening before the end of 2025 is functionally negligible, bounded at the 2% floor.
